name: Smithery

authentication.description: Smithery API key passed as Bearer token in Authorization header

create_server.notes: Idempotent - returns success if server already exists

fields.module: file (JavaScript module for hosted releases - optional)
fields.sourcemap: file (source map file for hosted releases - optional)
fields.bundle: file (MCPB bundle for stdio releases - optional)

publish_release.notes: Supports hosted (JS module), external (URL), and stdio (MCPB bundle) release types

cli_publish.notes: CLI also supports publishing with --config-schema for URL-based servers

notes: Smithery has a fully documented REST API at api.smithery.ai. The API requires authentication via Bearer token (API key). Two main endpoints exist: PUT /servers/{qualifiedName} to create/update a server, and PUT /servers/{qualifiedName}/releases to publish a release. A web form is also available at smithery.ai/new for manual URL-based submissions. Additionally, the CLI tool (@smithery/cli) supports publishing via 'smithery mcp publish'. The API supports three release types: hosted (JS module upload), external (URL-based), and stdio (MCPB bundle). OpenAPI spec available at https://app.stainless.com/api/spec/documented/smithery/openapi.documented.yml. Official JS SDK available (@smithery/api).
